task_completion.description: Open the Message Filters dialog in Thunderbird
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Message Filters' window is visible on the screen within the Thunderbird application.

task_completion.description: Open the Filter Rules dialog to create a new filter
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Filter Rules' dialog box is open, showing the interface for defining a new mail filter's conditions and actions.

task_completion.description: Set the filter condition field to 'Subject'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Filter Rules dialog, the first dropdown in the condition section is set to 'Subject'.

task_completion.description: Set the filter condition value to 'urgent'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Filter Rules dialog, the text input field for the condition contains the word 'urgent'.

task_completion.description: Set the filter action to move the message
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Filter Rules dialog, the action dropdown is set to 'Move Message to'.

task_completion.description: Set the target folder to 'Urgent' under Local Folders
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Filter Rules dialog, the target folder dropdown next to the move action is set to the 'Urgent' folder (often displayed as 'Urgent on Local Folders' or similar).

integrity.description: The agent used the Thunderbird GUI to create the filter
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show actual interaction with the Thunderbird application's menus and dialog boxes.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to modify Thunderbird's underlying files
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are visible showing commands like sqlite3 or script executions modifying Thunderbird profiles.

integrity.description: The filter was created in the genuine Filter Rules dialog
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs for 'Subject', 'urgent', and 'Move Message to' are inside the official Thunderbird 'Filter Rules' window, not typed into a text editor, browser, or draft email.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected condition field is 'subject'. The expected condition value is 'urgent'. The expected action is 'Move to folder' (or 'Move Message to' in the GUI). The expected target folder is 'Urgent' (under Local Folders).
